title: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. (CRWD) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of CrowdStrike (CRWD) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 28% growth rate is highly optimistic but necessary given its massive P/FCF of 87.47. The company possesses extraordinary growth momentum, expanding from endpoint security into cloud security and identity protection, driving substantial software-as-a-service free cash flow leverage.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8% discount rate reflects CrowdStrike's status as a top-tier, large-cap software company with minimal debt, immense recurring revenue streams, and high barriers to exit for existing enterprise customers.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 4% terminal growth rate is used, acknowledging that cybersecurity spending will likely remain a persistent and critical, long-term requirement that structurally outpaces the global economy.

thesisTitle: The Cyber Moat: Essential But Priced For Perfection
thesisParagraphs: CrowdStrike has fundamentally changed the cybersecurity landscape. Its Falcon platform, leveraging cloud-native architecture and massive data network effects, provides endpoint security that scales seamlessly and intelligently across the largest global enterprises. The July 2024 software update outage demonstrated not only the vast reach of its software but also the extreme difficulty enterprises face in abandoning it. The company's expansion into identity and cloud security creates an increasingly sticky and comprehensive ecosystem.

However, at a price of $428.18 and an astronomical free cash flow multiple nearing 90, the market is pricing in near-flawless execution and hyper-growth for the foreseeable future. While CrowdStrike is undeniably an exceptional business with high recurring revenues and dominant market share, the valuation leaves virtually no margin of safety for macroeconomic shocks, intense competition from Microsoft, or potential reputational setbacks. It is a phenomenal company trading at an exorbitant premium.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via Finviz (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 28% free cash flow growth rate?
faq.answer: CrowdStrike operates a highly scalable SaaS model. As customers adopt more modules on the Falcon platform, incremental margins expand significantly. A 28% rate attempts to capture this rapid cash flow acceleration over the next five years.

faq.question: Did the 2024 software outage affect this valuation?
faq.answer: While the incident caused short-term volatility, the intrinsic value model focuses on long-term cash flow generation. The rapid recovery and continued customer adoption suggest the structural moat remains intact, though it highlights the reputational risks inherent in the business.

faq.question: Why is the verdict 'OVERVALUED' despite strong fundamentals?
faq.answer: The valuation model suggests that even with highly optimistic growth projections (28% annually), the current market price of $428.18 fully bakes in these expectations and more, resulting in a negative margin of safety.
